hero.title: JOSOOR
hero.subtitle: Transformation Intelligence
hero.sectors: Operating System for the Public Sector
hero.badge:
Delivery at the Speed of Ambition
Vision 2030
hero.pitch: Public sector leaders must navigate two opposing forces while maintaining strict regulatory accountability and governance:
pitchPoints: The national mandate to rapidly scale AI adoption and digital transformation
The inherent risk of relying on probabilistic, unguided AI models for strategic decisions
bestPracticesPoints: Establish a deterministic enterprise architecture that orchestrates and governs AI behavior
Constrain AI to specific, high-value cognitive tasks equipped with verifiable data tools
hero.intelligenceDesc: Rapid analysis of complex relations, enriched with context
hero.trustDesc: Consistent outputs you can depend on and trace in full
hero.middleTagline: Building the bridges from Strategy to Execution, one bridge at a time.

aitoia.title: The AI Adoption Gap in the Enterprise
aitoia.subtitle: Successful integration requires architectural readiness, not just technological acquisition
aitoia.challengeSubtitle: How can institutions scale intelligence while maintaining structural integrity?
reasons: Data is not ready - Years of silos, both structured and unstructured
Lack of business value - Chasing the hype vs solving business problems
Escalating total cost of ownership - Underestimating running investments
Poor change management - AI is still viewed as a replacement tool
Responsible AI is an afterthought - causing incidents that erode credibility
aitoia.consequence: Ignoring AI means falling behind. Adopting it without structure means unmanaged risk. The question isn't whether to act — but how.
aitoia.innovationSubtitle: We design cognitive frameworks tailored to the unique operational reality of each institution. True AI adoption starts with a strategic alignment of data, operational context, and organizational mandate.

iaItems.desc: Context is everything. Knowing your operating model reality from data, systems, to adoption paints a picture.
Different entities are at different stages of their journeys. We align with these dynamic priorities.
Between what's possible and what's needed is a set of high-impact use cases.

claims.title: See the difference
claims.subtitle: What makes JOSOOR fundamentally different
claims.canvasLabel2: From Black Box to Glass Box
claims.canvasDesc: Every structure is machine-native. Every interface is human-intuitive.
imageDescs: Agents draw from a curated Vision 2030 knowledge base spanning all sectors through a dedicated MCP Server. Every query combines national priorities with your entity's own data — decisions are never made in a vacuum. The result: recommendations that are aligned by design, not by afterthought.

The first real solution to Bridging Strategy to Execution. Sectors, capabilities, KPIs, risks, and projects — all connected in one living model. Risk governance is built into the structure, not bolted on. This is the IP that makes everything else work.

Three Master Agents in a continuous loop:|Observe: Are we on track? Strategic PMO.|Decide: Risks caught early, prioritized in one click. Risk Specialist.|Execute: Actionable plan before the meeting ends. Planning Specialist.

Human-First Architecture with AI-in-the-Loop for deterministic decision-making. Agents are powered by a 4-layer memory, a scalable Vision database, specialized MCP tools, and a source-of-truth World Model. Every output is traceable. Every recommendation is explainable.
Homegrown in KSA. Model-agnostic. No vendor lock-in. Full sovereignty from day one.|Your data never leaves your infrastructure. Your AI answers to your rules, not someone else's terms of service.

solution.title: Five Questions. Clear Answers.
solution.subtitle: Before adopting any AI system, every leader asks the same questions. Here are ours — with nothing hidden.
solution.block1Question: What can AI do for my transformation?
solution.block1Answer: Forget superficial tools. True institutional AI reimagines existing workflows—connecting how you observe the sector, make decisions, and deliver value. By establishing a unified cognitive graph, your strategy, risk, delivery, and intelligence are bound into a single living model. This shifts analysis from weeks to minutes, anchoring every executive decision to verifiable context.

block1List.text: One operating environment for every role — leadership, PMO, risk, planning — with real-time views tailored to their mandate.
Goals, capabilities, risks, projects, and KPIs — all linked. Change one, see the ripple across everything.
Not static charts. Dashboards that detect anomalies, flag risks, and suggest interventions — before you ask.
AI-generated, Arabic-native, traceable to source. No more month-end scramble.

solution.block1Ev1Caption: The Josoor desktop — a unified workspace where leadership, PMO, and planning teams each see what matters to them, powered by one connected model.
solution.block1Ev2Caption: The enterprise ontology — goals, capabilities, risks, and projects connected in a living graph. Change one node, see the impact everywhere.
solution.block1Ev3Caption: Leadership-ready reports generated by AI in Arabic — traceable, explainable, and produced in minutes instead of weeks.
solution.block2Question: AI guesses. Government decisions cannot. How do you solve this?
solution.block2Answer: Traditional AI generates the most probable answer. In public sector work, probable is not acceptable — decisions must be traceable, explainable, and auditable. Intelligence should never operate in a vacuum. Every recommendation must be grounded in a rigorous methodology mapping your sector: goals, capabilities, risks, and projects. The AI reasons over this verified, structured reality, eliminating the risk of unconstrained hallucination.
solution.block2Quote: Every output traces to a source. Every recommendation is explainable. No black boxes.
solution.block2Ev1Caption: Full trace of every AI conversation — model used, tokens consumed, tool calls made, sources consulted, and response details. Nothing is hidden. Every decision the AI makes is logged and auditable.
solution.block3Question: Others tried and failed. Maybe the technology is not mature enough. Should I wait?
solution.block3Answer: The technology is ready. The implementations were not. Over 50% of enterprise AI projects fail¹ — not because the AI is wrong, but because five foundational problems were never addressed. We solve them before writing a single line of AI.

block3List.text: We build the knowledge structure first — before any AI touches it.
Every AI capability maps to a real operational need, not a technology showcase.
Modular architecture. Scale what works, stop what doesn't.
Human-first, AI-in-the-loop. Your team controls the flow and gets AI support where and when it matters. Not autonomous.
Transparency, accountability, and explainability are not features we add later — they are enforced by the architecture. Humans gate every AI action.

solution.block4Question: Will my services stay online? Can anyone shut us down?
solution.block4Answer: No one can. This is not a SaaS subscription that someone revokes. The entire system runs on your infrastructure — on-premise or local cloud. The AI engine is modular: swap it anytime, no rebuilding required. Your data never leaves your environment under any scenario. We work with you to build two things: your own system, and your own internal capability to run it. It does not work any other way.
solution.block5Question: There are many AI vendors. How do we ensure strategic alignment?
solution.block5Answer: Two criteria that define successful integration:

block5List.text: The initial phases require structural discovery—understanding actual operational needs. Large platforms force generic workflows upon you. You need an architecture that intimately adapts to your reality.
Institutional intelligence must understand its environment. Our approach is grounded in Vision 2030 knowledge architecture, seamlessly weaving strategy-to-execution in an Arabic-first paradigm.

visionMemory.title: Complexity As Your Most Valuable Asset
visionMemory.subtitle: 38 official publications. 150+ government entities. Thousands of targets and KPIs — previously distributed across myriad sources, now unified into a single navigable reality.
visionMemory.description: We mapped thousands of Vision 2030 targets, KPIs, and policies from publicly available sources across multiple government entities, structuring them into a unified cognitive graph. Traditional dashboards and the relational databases that power them are highly effective at retrieving information, but they are inherently built against the known. They are designed to answer predefined questions, yet offer little when it comes to navigating for the purpose of discovery. In contrast, a cognitive model is built for exploration—empowering leaders to navigate complex cross-sector dependencies and uncover hidden relationships, guided entirely by the bold questions they ask. Consequently, when this methodology is applied within an organization, internal data can be interconnected in the exact same manner alongside the broader context of the Vision. Thus, transforming the complexity of your data into a strategic advantage and laying the foundation for true institutional intelligence.
visionMemory.statLabel: Connected Data Points from 38 Official Sources

highlights.text: Shifting the paradigm from retrieving static metrics to actively exploring hidden dependencies and structural relationships.
Intelligently linked to the underlying sectors, entities, and execution policies that drive their success.
Trace exactly how a strategic shift in one sector ripples across others, spanning 150+ government entities based on publicly available data.

convergence.query: Identify cross-sector collaboration opportunities the Vision missed that can bring acceleration with minimal cost. Focus on top opportunities that can be deployed ASAP
convergence.result1: Asset Life Cycle Management — 19 departments building it independently
convergence.result2: Infrastructure Planning — 54 departments stuck at Level 1, while 3 are already at Level 5
convergence.result3: Smart Reporting Tool — active in some sectors, planned from scratch in 12 others
convergence.resultFooter: Zero new budget required. Export what already works.
convergence.discoveryNote: This answer was not pre-built. The graph discovered it.
convergence.followUp: I have a few follow up questions about the infrastructure planning gap...

strategyHouse.title: Trusted Frameworks on Intelligent Foundations
strategyHouse.subtitle: Transforming Complexity into your most valuable asset
elementDescs: Transforming a sector means changing how it works — who invests, who operates, and what they focus on. Governments steer this through policy and regulation, incentives, and awareness. JOSOOR maps these tools to the players, investments, and activities they influence — so you see which levers are moving the sector toward national objectives and which ones are not.

You cannot transform a sector without transforming the entity that manages it. The capabilities needed to design policies, enforce regulations, and run incentive programs must be built in sync with the sector's evolution. JOSOOR tracks both in parallel — so your operating model is always ready for the mandate it serves.

Too many initiatives, limited resources, and high stakes. Portfolio management is how you maximize impact from every investment while containing risk. JOSOOR scores ROI across projects, assets, capabilities, and leadership time — so you know where to double down and where to stop spending.

Agents draw from a curated Vision 2030 knowledge base spanning all sectors through a dedicated MCP Server. Every query combines national priorities with your entity's own data — decisions are never made in a vacuum. The result: recommendations that are aligned by design, not by afterthought.

The first real solution to Bridging Strategy to Execution. Sectors, capabilities, KPIs, risks, and projects — all connected in one living model. Risk governance is built into the structure, not bolted on. This is the IP that makes everything else work.

Three Master Agents in a continuous loop:|Observe: Are we on track? Strategic PMO.|Decide: Risks caught early, prioritized in one click. Risk Specialist.|Execute: Actionable plan before the meeting ends. Planning Specialist.

Human-First Architecture with AI-in-the-Loop for deterministic decision-making. Agents are powered by a 4-layer memory, a scalable Vision database, specialized MCP tools, and a source-of-truth World Model. Every output is traceable. Every recommendation is explainable.
Homegrown in KSA. Model-agnostic. No vendor lock-in. Full sovereignty from day one.|Your data never leaves your infrastructure. Your AI answers to your rules, not someone else's terms of service.

founderLetter.title: Founder's Letter: Origins
paragraphs: Two years ago, a weekend experiment with GenAI changed everything. What began as asking ChatGPT to design a training program ended with a revelation: unlocking human potential lies not in chasing answers, but in articulating the right questions. That weekend gave birth to the Living Transformation Network — a constellation of AIs with one mission: absorb the complexity of society so we can dare to think.
"What do we do with all the spare time we will have?"
I published that wild interaction on LinkedIn, hoping to nudge skeptics and undecided leaders to take the leap.

The core question stayed with me: how do you make transformation management tame the complexity beast? I spent six months designing the model — a relational architecture that captured every moving part. But the technology wasn't ready. Building it meant the old playbook: licenses, infrastructure, armies of coders.
So I kept it on a slow burner, refining it against real client setups I encountered.

Then, months ago, the landscape shifted. GenAI capabilities surged, no-code tooling matured, and suddenly the firepower to navigate a complex web of relations was not only available — it was remarkably affordable. Cloud hosting broke through too, with Azure earning government certification after years of a no-cloud policy.
No more excuses. No more waiting.

Throughout my career I pioneered for others. This was my chance to pioneer without constraints — fuelled, honestly, by years of bottled-up red-tape frustration. I am pursuing this all the way, Inshallah.
I hope once the concept and its national benefits click, you will join me — whether as an Architect or a Builder — so we give it the best chance of success.

controls.flagRiskConcentration: PT3: Pricing Framework - RISK CONCENTRATION (3+ risks >50%)
controls.flagRiskBroken: PT1: Desalination Policy - BROKEN GOVERNANCE (exposure 70%, inactive link)
controls.flagDeliverySaturation: Cap1: Plant Operations - SATURATION (5+ active projects)
controls.flagDeliveryBottleneck: Adopt3: QC Change - ADOPTION BOTTLENECK (3+ projects, <1 adoption)
controls.flagIntegrityBroken: Obj3: Service Quality - BROKEN CHAIN (30% leakage)
controls.flagIntegritySink: Cap2: Distribution - SINK CAPABILITY (35% leakage, high inflow, low outflow)

ont_executive_prompt: Based on this data, produce a comprehensive executive report on sector health: where are the biggest exposures? What are the top 3 urgent priorities this quarter? And what is the minister-level summary?
ont_column_prompt: Based on this data, analyze "{{colName}}": what is its current standing? What are the key issues? What dependencies affect it? And what are the actionable recommendations?
ont_micro_category: Category: {{label}} | Status: {{status}} | Total: {{count}} | Critical: {{redCount}} | At Risk: {{amberCount}}
ont_micro_item_detail: Item: {{name}} | ID: {{id}} | Status: {{rag}} | Depends on: {{upLinks}} | Affects: {{downLinks}}
ont_impact_cascade: "{{name}}" impacts {{impact}} downstream KPIs — unresolved, this will cascade across sector outputs
ont_no_data_for_node: No data available for this node
ont_from_no_dest: {{count}} {{fromLbl}} with no destination + {{bastards}} {{toLbl}} with no source
ont_micro_landscape_prompt: In 3-4 short bullets: Why is {{label}} in this state? What is the single biggest risk right now? What should be done first?
ont_micro_block1_prompt: In 3-4 short bullets: What connects these top issues? Which one, if fixed, would improve the others? What is the recommended sequence of action?
ont_micro_item_prompt: In 3-4 short bullets: Why is "{{name}}" flagged? What upstream dependencies are causing this? What is the downstream impact if left unresolved? What is the one action to take now?
